topics.topic: MEASURING INSTRUMENTS & THEIR CHARACTERISTICS
Define Metrology, Measurement and Standard of Measurement
Explain the Significance of Measurement
Classify and Explain the Methods of Measurement
Classify Type of errors
Describe Precision, Accuracy, Sensitivity, Hysteresis, Response Time, Repeatability, Calibration, Uncertainty of Measurement, and Interchangeability
Explain standardization and standardize organizations
List Measuring Instruments and Systems and explain their functions
Select the appropriate Measuring Instruments for a given application
Explain calibration of measuring instruments
List Types of Tachometers
Explain and illustrate the working principle of: ➢ Mechanical Tachometers – Revolution Counter and Timer, Slipping Clutch Tachometer. ➢ Electrical Tachometer

MEASURING INSTRUMENTS & THEIR CHARACTERISTICS Definition of Metrology, Define Measurement, Standard of measurement, Explain the Significance of Measurement, Explain Methods of Measurement a. Direct and Indirect, b. Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary, c. Contact and Non -contact, Types of Errors - Controllable and Random Errors, Precision, accuracy, sensitivity, hysteresis, response time, repeatability, calibration, uncertainty of measurement, interchangeability, Standardization and standardizing organizations, Classify Instruments, List the Instruments an d Measuring Systems - Their Functions, Selection of Measuring Instruments, Calibration of measuring instruments
LINEAR AND ANGULAR MEASUREMENT Linear Measurement
Describe the construction features and use of Steel Rule, Calipers, Surface Plate, Angle Plate, V-block
Describe the construction features and use of Vernier calipers, vernier height and depth gauges, micrometres
Explain slip gauges, Cylinder bore gauges, feeler, wire gauges, Telescopic Gauge, Gauge Blocks. Checking flatness, roundness, and squareness Angular Measurement
Describe the construction features and use of instruments for angular measurements: bevel protector, sine bar, angle gauges
List Types of Thermometers
Explain Bimetallic Thermometer
Explain Thermocouple
Explain Thermistor
Describe and Compare Pyrometers: Radiation and Optical

LINEAR AND ANGULAR MEASUREMENT Linear Measurement Construction features and use of instruments for non-precision linear measurement: steel rule, callipers, surface plate, angle plate, V -block, Construction features and use of instruments for precision measurements: vernier calipers, vernier height and dep th gauges, micrometers, Slip gauges, Indian standards of slip gauges, sets of slip gauges, use of slip gauges. Cylinder bore gauges, feeler, and wire gauges, Telescopic Gauge, Gauge Blocks. Checking flatness, roundness, and squareness. Angular Measurement - Construction features and use of instruments for angular measurements: bevel protector, sine bar, angle gauges. Opto -mechanical measurements for angular Measurement: Tool Maker’s Microscope
DIMENSIONAL AND SURFACE METROLOGY
Explain Limits, Fits, and Tolerances
Define Terminology of Surface Roughness
Illustrate the Concept of Primary Texture and Secondary Texture
Analyze Factors Affecting Surface Finish
Calculate CLA, RMS, and Ra Values
Describe and Demonstrate Stylus Probe Instruments – Taylor Surface Talysurf

DIMENSIONAL AND SURFACE METROLOGY Limits, Fits, and Tolerance, Terminology of surface roughness, Concept of primary texture and secondary texture, Factors affecting surface finish, CLA, RMS, and Ra value, Principle and operation of stylus probe instruments. Taylor surface talysurf
TRANSDUCERS
Define Transducer
Classify Transducers: Active & Passive, Analog & Digital
Explain Mechanical Detector elements: Mass Sensing Elements, Thermal Detectors (basic idea only)
Explain Resistive, Piezoelectric, and Capacitive Transducers
Define Strain Gauges
List and Describe the Requirements of a Strain Gauge
Explain Types of Strain Gauges

TRANSDUCERS Define Transducer, Classification of Transducers (Active & Passive, Analog & Digital), elastic and force balance transducers. Mechanical Detector Elements: Mass Sensing Elements, Thermal Detectors (basic idea only). Explain the Resistive Transducer, the Piezoelectric Transducer, and the Capacitive Transducer. Define Strain Gauges, List the requirements of a strain gauge, Explain the types of strain gauges: unbounded metal strain gauges, bonded metal wire strain gauges, bonded metal foil strain gauges, bonded semiconductor strain gauges
MEASUREMENT OF ANGULAR SPEED, TEMPERATURE, PRESSURE, AND FLOW
Measurement of angular speed
Measurement of Temperature
List Pressure Measurement Devices and Explain Bourdon Tube Pressure Gauge

MEASUREMENT OF ANGULAR SPEED, TEMPERATURE, PRESSURE, AND FLOW Measurement of angular speed: List the types of Tachometers, explain the working principle of the following Tachometers with a legible sketch: Mechanical Tachometers - Revolution counter and timer, slipping Clutch Tachometer. Electrical / electronic tachometers, Stroboscopes, Incrementa l and absolute encoders, Laser Doppler velocimeters / interferometers. Measurement of Temperature: List the types of thermometers, explain the Bimetallic thermometer, Thermocouples, thermistors, Bimetallic thermometers, Infrar ed pyrometers, and thermal cameras, Fiber optic temperature sensors, Calibration, range, linearization, and error sources. Explain Pyrometers-Radiation and Optical. Measurement of Pressure: List the Types of Pressure Measurement Devices. Explain the Bourdon tube Pressure gauge, Manometers, Elastic and force balance transducers, Strain gauge pressure cells, Piezoelectric, piezo resistive, and capacitive pressure sensors, High -pressure and low -pressure measurement techniques, Applications for mechanical and industrial systems. Measurement of Flow: List the Types of Flow meters- Explain Rota meters, hot wire anemometer (constant temp and constant Current), and an Ultrasonic flow meter REFERENCE BOOKS 1. Mechanical Measurements &Control -Dr.. D. S. Kumar (Metropolitan Book Co. Pvt.Ltd) 2. Mechanical & Industrial Measurements - R.K. Jain 3. Engineering Metrology and Measurements – N.V. Raghavendra & L. Krishnamurthy 4. A Textbook of Engineering Metrology – I.C. Gupta 5. Metrology and Measurement– Anand Bewoor & Vinay Kulkarni Table specifying the scope of syllabus to be covered for Unit Test-I & Unit Test-II Unit Test Learning Outcomes to be covered Unit Test – I From 1.1 to 3.3 Unit Test – II From 3.4 to 5.4
